summary:
You are given a collection of intervals, each written as [start, end]. Some of them may overlap or touch.

Combine every group of overlapping intervals into a single interval, and return a list of the merged, non-overlapping intervals.

examples.explanation: [1, 3] and [2, 6] overlap and merge into [1, 6]; the other two stand alone.
The intervals touch at 4, so they merge into [1, 5].

constraints: Each interval is [start, end] with start <= end
Intervals that only share an endpoint are treated as overlapping and should be merged
The returned list may be in any order, but each interval must be non-overlapping
